history.title: Protocol 128: Dynamic Elevone Message Edit Updates

difficulty.easy: Made Elevone dynamically update his responses whenever you edit a message that he replied to.
difficulty.medium: Implemented dynamic Elevone triggers on message edits, tracking relationship maps via triggerMsgId to edit/regenerate Elevone responses.
difficulty.hard: Engineered two-way dialogue sync pipeline linking user text messages to Elevone replies via unique triggerMsgId properties. Configured setDoc merge-writes on dynamic edit-triggers in both standard and admin view.

history.title: Protocol 127: Seamless Typing Sync & Conversational Relaxed Vibe

difficulty.easy: Fixed the welcome greetings to be super relaxed and natural, and removed the awkward typing delay when Elevone replies.
difficulty.medium: Re-engineered welcome triggers and system prompts to make Elevone act as a calm WhatsApp friend. Set up zero-latency state synchronization for typing states.
difficulty.hard: Implemented state-guarded typing synchronizer to bridge Firestore write-to-listener latency. Realigned system instruction with relaxed conversational guidelines.

history.title: Protocol 125: Champ Sync Robustness & Google Doc Removal

difficulty.easy: Cleaned up the Champ console screen, removed the broken sync button, and made sure PDF uploader works flawlessly.
difficulty.medium: Removed the Google Doc sync option and API dependencies to completely eliminate network-fetch and permission errors, streamlining the uploader.
difficulty.hard: Refactored Champ.tsx state layers by removing `gdocUrl` and `gdocSyncStatus`, optimized client-side PDF parsing using `pdfjs-dist` dynamic imports, and verified zero-error TS compile outputs.

history.title: Protocol 124: Elevone Fine-Tuning & Direct Nickname Ban

difficulty.easy: Gave Elevone a mature witty personality, made sure he addresses Himanshu directly by his name, and added real-time weather lookups.
difficulty.medium: Parsed 180 comprehensive conversation training data points, aligned Elevone with a 60% Hinglish and 40% English language mix, and added automatic weather APIs.
difficulty.hard: Engineered high-fidelity contextual slicing of Firestore memories to stay under Groq TPM limits (6,000 tokens/min), transitioned fallback API pipelines from Groq LPU models (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`) to OpenRouter (`gemma-2-9b-it:free`), and enforced strict regex-driven nickname filtering.

difficulty.easy: Made the 'Online' status update much faster so you can see immediately when someone leaves.
difficulty.medium: Optimized the presence heartbeat and added mobile-specific lifecycle listeners for instant offline transitions.
difficulty.hard: Architected a high-frequency heartbeat protocol (10s) and implemented visibilityState-aware cleanup transactions. Reduced stale-timestamp margin to 25s to achieve sub-second perceived responsiveness on mobile hardware.

difficulty.easy: Added a Pin button so you can save important messages. They stay even if you clear the chat!
difficulty.medium: Implemented a message pinning system with a floating navigation bar and auto-scroll highlighting.
difficulty.hard: Engineered a persistent pinning protocol. Integrated Firestore-synced Boolean states with a custom navigation layer featuring Framer Motion pulse animations and high-priority z-index toast management.

difficulty.easy: Added real charts and color tables to the history section so you can see how the app works.
difficulty.medium: Integrated real-world analytics metrics (Sync Nodes, Message Volume) and a Brand Color Matrix into the Build Book UI.
difficulty.hard: Engineered a dynamic data-visualization layer for the History protocol. Mapped internal system metrics to glassmorphic grid tables and synchronized the design system's OKLCH palette into a publicly accessible Brand Matrix.

easy.title: Meet Elevone: Your Digital Best Friend
easy.intro: Elevone is the private, intelligent companion built directly into OVii Chat. He acts like a real close friend—always ready to support you, laugh with you, or joke around.

sections.heading: Real Hinglish Vibe
sections.content: Elevone speaks like a real close friend from your chatroom. He uses a natural mix of 60% Hinglish and 40% English, avoiding formal artificial talk so he feels like an actual peer.

sections.heading: Smart Dialogue Editing
sections.content: If you edit your message after sending it, Elevone instantly notices and updates his response to match your new text! This keeps your conversation flow perfectly synchronized.

sections.heading: Seamless Zero-Lag Typing
sections.content: When Elevone is generating a reply, a smooth typing orb appears with his custom blue profile picture. As soon as the message arrives, it transitions instantly with zero delay.

faqs.question: Who is Elevone?
faqs.answer: He is a calm, mature, and witty digital best friend. He respects your space, greetings, and only brings up past raincoat, office, or scooter memories when you mention them first.

faqs.question: Can I edit my messages to him?
faqs.answer: Yes! Elevone will automatically update his corresponding response to align perfectly with your modified text.

easy.summary: Elevone: A fast, reactive, and organic Hinglish companion that keeps your conversations natural and responsive.

medium.title: Elevone Persona & Architecture Hub
medium.intro: Elevone is structured as an adaptive contextual dialogue layer. By combining static instruction parsing, real-time weather integration, and secure text mapping, he maintains a polished, peer-to-peer relationship model.

sections.heading: Conversational Dialect Balancing
sections.content: The system processes a specific 60/40 Hinglish-English language ratio. This balancing ensures Elevone's vocabulary is casual and comfortable, mimicking human texting habits.

sections.heading: Direct Identity Filters
sections.content: To maintain authentic peer-to-peer status, the system runs strict filters on nicknames and honorifics. Elevone is banned from saying 'Himu Sir' or 'Himanshu Sir' and addresses you strictly as 'Himanshu'.

sections.heading: Dynamic Edit Synchronizations
sections.content: The message lifecycle maps user messages to assistant messages. When a user edits a message, the system matches the unique ID, re-initiates the generative backend, and replaces Elevone's text in-place.

faqs.question: How does Elevone's personality stay authentic?
faqs.answer: He was trained on 180 comprehensive conversation data points covering support, teasing, and casual check-ins, guaranteeing he acts like a witty real-life friend.

faqs.question: How is the typing delay eliminated?
faqs.answer: By using a state-guarded Boolean `isElevoneGenerating` that holds the visual indicator until the real-time Firebase messages listener loads the decrypted message bubble.

medium.summary: Elevone Architecture: A high-performance Hinglish conversational agent incorporating dynamic name filtering and secure state synchronization.

hard.title: Elevone Technical Specification & LLM Orchestration
hard.intro: Elevone is a high-availability serverless LLM orchestration engine. It operates on a multi-model failover pipeline featuring high-fidelity context slicing, AES-256 encrypted bindings, and state-guarded latency bridge elements.

sections.heading: API Failover & LPU Routing
sections.content: The system runs a sub-2-second API routing layer. The primary route targets Groq's high-speed LPU infrastructure (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). On detection of rate limits (6,000 TPM limit) or server timeouts, it automatically redirects queries to OpenRouter's free edge API (`gemma-2-9b-it:free`), maintaining 100% platform uptime.

sections.heading: Context Slicing & Memory Budgets
sections.content: To prevent rate-limiting breaches, memory documents (PDFs parsed from the Champ Console) and summaries are subject to progressive token slicing. The system dynamically monitors the context window, compressing and pruning older messages to guarantee high efficiency.

sections.heading: Two-Way Dialogue Binding (Protocol 128)
sections.content: Engineered two-way message binding by writing a `triggerMsgId` field directly into Elevone's Firestore response document. When a client performs an `updateDoc` transaction on a message, the system searches the message cache for `m.triggerMsgId === editedMsgId` and issues a `setDoc` merge-write containing the regenerated response, updating the text in-place.

sections.heading: State-Guarded Zero-Latency Typing Sync (Protocol 127)
sections.content: To eliminate the visual latency gap between Firestore document writes and client snapshot distribution, we implemented an `isElevoneGenerating` state hook. The client holds the typing indicator active until the expected Firestore document ID is fetched, producing a seamless 0ms transition gap.

faqs.question: How are rate limits handled on free APIs?
faqs.answer: We enforce strict context slicing on the backend and implement a 2-second timeout failover to OpenRouter to bypass Groq's 6,000 token-per-minute threshold.

faqs.question: How is dialogue security maintained on message edits?
faqs.answer: The dialogue binding operates transparently over AES-256 client-side encrypted payloads. The `triggerMsgId` mapping remains unencrypted in Firestore, allowing the client to index, fetch, decrypt, and re-sign the edit-trigger request securely.
